topic: wet harvest conditions and grape dilution

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Substantial rain or persistently wet conditions occur close to harvest.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Berries can absorb water and swell, increasing juice volume relative to dissolved sugars, acids, and flavour compounds.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Grape components may be diluted, reducing sugar and acid concentration and potentially weakening flavour intensity; disease pressure also rises.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Grape components may be diluted, reducing sugar and acid concentration and potentially weakening flavour intensity; disease pressure also rises.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
